name: Current Affairs in Indian Economy & TN Dev. Admin. (7): India's 2026 Semiconductor Plant Inaugurations

sourceRefs.label: investors.micron.com/swarajyamag.com/republicworld.com - Micron Sanand ATMP (28 Feb 2026); Kaynes Semicon Sanand (31 Mar 2026); Rajasthan's first semiconductor facility, Bhiwadi (15 May 2026); Tata Electronics-ASML agreement (16 May 2026)

researchNotes: Eighth Economy current-affairs topic (7th lap). Directly extends the Day-66 'India Semiconductor Mission 2.0' mention with 4 concrete, dated 2026 plant inaugurations/agreements — showing the Mission moving from policy announcement to physical execution. Expansion pass (Batch 20): added the front-end-vs-back-end manufacturing distinction, explaining why ATMP/OSAT facilities came online before India's first front-end fab.

reading.intro: Covers 4 major 2026 milestones in India's semiconductor manufacturing buildout, all under the India Semiconductor Mission framework.

qa.question: What did Micron Technology inaugurate on 28 February 2026, and where?
qa.answer: India's first semiconductor Assembly, Test, and Packaging (ATMP) facility, at Sanand, Gujarat — a ~$2.75 billion investment converting DRAM/NAND wafers from Micron's global network into finished memory/storage products.

qa.question: What did Kaynes Semicon inaugurate on 31 March 2026, also at Sanand, and what is its capacity?
qa.answer: An OSAT (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test) facility, focused on chip testing and packaging — investment of ₹33 billion (~$347.9 million), with a production capacity of around 6 million chips per day.

qa.question: Which state entered India's semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem for the first time on 15 May 2026, and under which scheme?
qa.answer: Rajasthan — with its first semiconductor ATMP/OSAT facility at Bhiwadi, developed under the central government's SPECS (Scheme for Promotion of Manufacturing of Electronic Components and Semiconductors) scheme.

qa.question: What major front-end fabrication agreement was signed on 16 May 2026, and what does it involve?
qa.answer: Tata Electronics signed a strategic agreement with Netherlands-based ASML to support India's first front-end semiconductor fabrication facility — a 300mm wafer fab being developed at Dholera, Gujarat, backed by an estimated $11 billion investment; ASML will supply advanced manufacturing technology and equipment.

qa.question: What is the difference between 'front-end' and 'back-end' (ATMP/OSAT) semiconductor manufacturing, based on this topic's facilities?
qa.answer: FRONT-END (fabrication/'fab') = growing and processing raw silicon wafers into chips — the most technologically complex and capital-intensive stage (Tata-ASML's Dholera fab). BACK-END (ATMP/OSAT) = assembling, testing, and packaging already-fabricated chips into finished products (Micron's Sanand facility, Kaynes' Sanand facility) — less complex than front-end fabrication, which is why India's ATMP/OSAT facilities came online before its first front-end fab.

questions.prompt: Micron Technology's ATMP facility — India's first such facility — is located at:
Kaynes Semicon's OSAT facility (inaugurated 31 March 2026) has a production capacity of approximately:
Rajasthan's first semiconductor facility (Bhiwadi, inaugurated 15 May 2026) was developed under which scheme?
Tata Electronics signed a strategic agreement with which company (16 May 2026) for India's first front-end semiconductor fab?
Tata Electronics' front-end semiconductor fab (with ASML) is being developed at Dholera, Gujarat, with an estimated investment of:

options: Growing and processing raw silicon wafers into chips
questions.explanation: Growing and processing raw silicon wafers into chips.
questions.trick: Front-end = making the chip from raw silicon; back-end (ATMP/OSAT) = assembling/testing/packaging an already-made chip.
